title: Derive or kill the constraint propagation closure law for A/A_spatial
description: Constraint propagation is the nearest surviving bridge from A to A_spatial. A legitimate closure must: derive A_spatial, preserve source/current conservation, recover gamma-like and AB checks, and maintain count-once trace theorem. If no non-GR closure law exists, the A-sector-local branch is killed and the search moves to action/stiffness or conservation/Bianchi-like identities.
